commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): create .claude.json to prevent auth re-confirmation

Add creation of ~/.claude/.claude.json in postCreateCommand.
Without this file, Claude Code treats the environment as a new
installation and requests re-authentication on every container rebuild.

Refs #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix session status not updating in project session list (#193)

## Summary
- Terminal SSE events (`session_completed`, `session_failed`,
`session_stopped`) were published without `project_id`, so the
frontend's `useGlobalSSE` hook could not invalidate the project-specific
session query cache (`["projects", id, "sessions"]`)
- Added `project_id` to `resolve_session_dir` return value,
`_publish_terminal` helper, and all `SessionEvent` publishes in the
`stop_session` endpoint
- Updated all call sites in `logs.py`, `sse.py`, and `sessions.py`
routers to unpack the new 3-tuple

## Test plan
- [ ] Launch a session from a project, wait for it to complete — verify
the project session list updates status without manual refresh
- [ ] Stop a running session — verify the project session list reflects
"stopped" status
- [ ] Check the Dashboard (global session list) still updates correctly
- [ ] Verify SSE events in browser devtools include `project_id` for all
lifecycle events

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(25): move cloud approval resolve callback outside SolidJS store

SolidJS stores wrap values in proxies for reactivity, which can break
function references. The pendingCloudRequestResolve callback stored in
the store was silently failing when called — the approval modal would
collapse but the promise never resolved, so the cloud request never sent.

Fix: store the resolve function in a plain module-level variable and
expose resolvePendingCloudRequest() helper for Shell.tsx to call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: sliding window attention, Gemma 3 config, and recipes

New attention variant: SlidingWindowGQA — restricts each token to
attend only within a configurable window_size. Registered as
"sliding_window_gqa" in attention_registry.

New model: Gemma 3 config factory with interleaved local/global
attention (every 6th layer is global GQA, rest are sliding window).
Uses per-layer block_configs for the mixed attention pattern.

BlockConfig gains: window_size field for sliding window control.

Recipes:
- train_tiny_gpt.py: end-to-end training on Shakespeare text
- compare_architectures.py: GPT vs LLaMA vs DeepSeek comparison

fix: use plain URL strings for Metricool media field

Metricool API requires media as plain URL strings ['https://...'],
not objects [{url, type}]. The object format caused "You need to add
a picture" errors at publish time despite 201 responses. Also updated
lessons.md with ephemeral storage, caption format, and media format findings.

   - 429 rate limit returns null gracefully

Standardise license setup: MPL-2.0 + PMPL-1.0-or-later (REUSE)

- LICENSE: pure MPL-2.0 text for machine detection (GitHub, registries)
- LICENSES/MPL-2.0.txt: standard MPL-2.0 text
- LICENSES/PMPL-1.0-or-later.txt: Palimpsest License text
- NOTICE: explains the dual-license relationship
- Source SPDX headers remain PMPL-1.0-or-later

This ensures OSI compliance checks pass while preserving PMPL as
the governing license for source code.

commit.message:
feat: v0.8 Grid Strategy Plugin + Portfolio Rebalancing

Grid Strategy:
- New grid strategy plugin (src/strategies/grid.ts) with arithmetic/geometric grid levels
- Auto-range detection from recent klines high/low with 2% padding
- populateSignal: buy at unfilled grid levels, sell above upper bound
- adjustPosition: DCA at lower unfilled levels with configurable USDT per grid
- shouldExit: grid take-profit at levels above entry
- onTradeClosed: recycles highest filled level for re-buy
- StateStore persistence for grid state across candles
- Wired stateStore into signal-engine.ts StrategyContext (fixes gap for all plugins)

Portfolio Rebalancing:
- New rebalance module (src/strategy/rebalance.ts) with target-weight deviation detection
- computeRebalanceOrders: generates buy/sell orders when weights deviate beyond threshold
- shouldRebalance: interval-based cooldown to prevent excessive rebalancing
- Integrated into both monitor.ts (paper) and live-monitor.ts (live) after portfolio exposure summary
- Sells execute before buys to free capital, orders capped at max_rebalance_ratio

Config: grid? on StrategyConfig.strategy + StrategyProfile.strategy, rebalance? on StrategyConfig
Tests: 37 new tests (23 grid + 14 rebalance), all 1630 existing tests pass

Split projection horizon into Investment + Operating phases

The single projection-years slider is replaced by two number inputs:
  🏗️  Investment Phase — pre-revenue construction / ramp-up period
  📈  Operating Phase  — revenue-generating operations
A live "Total" badge updates as either value changes.

Model changes:
- revenues.py: revenue = 0 for every investment-phase year; operating
  phase starts at base_revenue in Op Year 1 and grows from there
- dcf_model.py: period = investment_years + operating_years; both
  values are carried through in the results dict

Visual changes:
- tables.py: investment columns labelled "Inv N" with orange headers
  and amber cell tint; operating columns labelled "Op N" in green
- charts.py: phase-aware x-axis labels (Base / Inv N / Op N);
  investment bars coloured orange in the revenue chart

Sphere defaults updated with per-sector investment horizons:
  Production 1+5, Toll Road 3+12, Agriculture 2+10,
  Mining 3+12, Port 4+16

commit.message:
fix(site): markdown toggle collecting empty pageBody array

The #md-overlay div sits immediately after </nav>, so
nav.nextElementSibling === overlay. The while loop condition
`el !== overlay` caused immediate exit, leaving pageBody empty
and producing a blank codeblock. Fix: skip overlay inside the
loop instead of using it as a termination condition.

commit.message:
feat: add Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) and DeepSeek config

Implement MLA from DeepSeek V2/V3 — low-rank KV compression that
caches a compact latent vector instead of full KV heads. Supports
decoupled RoPE (rope_dim/nope_dim split) and optional Q compression.

New files:
- core/mla.py: MLA attention class registered as "mla"
- models/deepseek.py: DeepSeek config factory with deepseek_tiny()

BlockConfig gains: kv_lora_rank, q_lora_rank, rope_dim fields.

commit.message:
fix: Two critical Remotion fixes for smooth music video output

1. All-keyframe encode: re-encode merged.mp4 with -g 1 -keyint_min 1
   before passing to Remotion. Remotion seeks frame-by-frame — without
   all keyframes it produces choppy/stuttering output.

2. Lyrics word filter: filter transcript to only words at/after
   CHORUS_START before parseElevenLabsTranscript. Without this,
   pre-chorus words get clamped to t=0 and all appear at frame 0.

Both bugs confirmed and fixed via testing on espresso-sabrina.
